elements.section-2411-009: Dissipativity of the Linearization in Self-Similar Coordinates
elements.sent-2411-493: Throughout this section we fix $m \in \N$ with $m \geq 3$ and $k \in \Z$.
elements.sent-2411-494: Since we only deal with one operator at the same time we simplify the notation by setting $C := C_{m,k}$.
elements.sent-2411-495: We split \begin{equation}CU = MU+KU + (1-\tfrac{1}{\alpha}) U,\end{equation} Here $M$ is the main part and $K$ a kernel operator given by \begin{equation}M U(s) := \tfrac{1}{\alpha} U'(s)-mik\beta e^{-\alpha s}U(s), D(M) := \left\{ U \in L^2(R) \colon \tfrac{1}{\alpha} U'-mik\beta e^{-\alpha \bullet}U \in L^2(R) \right\}, KU(s) := -mik\alpha(2-\alpha)\beta e^{-\alpha s}\psi(s) , D(K) := \left\{ U \in L^2(R) \colon e^{-\alpha \bullet}\psi \in L^2(R) \right\} .\end{equation}.
